title: Moraea tristis
taxonomy: Moraea tristis is classified in the family Iridaceae, commonly known as the iris family. This family comprises over 200 genera and 2,000 species of flowering plants. Moraea tristis, being a member of this family, is a genus of flowering plants that are primarily found in Southern Africa. The genus Moraea is further divided into several sections, with Moraea tristis belonging to the section Moraea. The exact classification of Moraea tristis is further specified as Moraea tristis, with its full classification being Moraea tristis (D.Delaroche) Goldblatt.

intro: Moraea tristis, also known as the "sorrowful moraea" or "cape flower," is a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the management of certain infections. The bioactive constituents of Moraea tristis include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
